title: Samsung Electronics Announces Earnings Guidance for First Quarter 2026
content: Samsung Electronics today announced its earnings guidance for the first quarter of 2026. Consolidated Sales: Approximately 133 trillion Korean won Consolidated Operating Profit: Approximately 57.2 trillion Korean won The above estimates are based on K-IFRS. Please note that Korean disclosure regulations do not allow earnings estimates to be offered as a range. To comply with such regulations, the above figures represent the median of the estimate ranges provided below. Sales: 132 trillion to 134 trillion Korean won Operating Profit: 57.1 trillion to 57.3 trillion Korean won ※ 2025 4Q and 2025 1Q consolidated figures based on K-IFRS are as follows (in trillion won)2025.4Q2025.1QSales93.8479.14Operating profit20.076.69

title: AI Accelerators Usher In New Era Of Semiconductor Test
content:
The number and variety of test interfaces, coupled with increased packaging complexity, are creating an explosion of testing challenges.

title: Intel introduces its own Neural Compression technology with a fallback mode that works on GPUs without dedicated AI cores — early performance is on the level of Nvidia NTC
content: Intel is working on its own neural texture compression with similar compression performance to Nvidia's counterpart. Best of all, Intel has a fallback version of its compression tech that will work with GPUs that don't come with Intel's XMX engine.

title: Intel joins Elon Musk's TeraFab project — 'Intel is proud to join the Terafab project with SpaceX, xAI, and Tesla to help refactor silicon fab technology'
content: As Intel joins Elon Musk's TeraFab project, Lip-Bu Tan expects Elon Musk to reimagine the semiconductor industry.

title: Framework warns of even more rising RAM and SSD prices through 2026 as memory crisis persists — some reprieve as prices plateau in latest monthly update
content: The company is already adjusting pricing for high-capacity SSDs and memory-heavy configurations as supply costs increase.

title: Asus Zenbook A16 review: Snapdragon X2 Elite Extreme is a strong chip in a so-so package
content: The Asus Zenbook A16 is a lightweight housing for Qualcomm's Snapdragon X2 Elite Extreme, but comes with compromises in build quality and battery life.

title: Snag this 1440p iBuyPower gaming PC with a 7800X3D and RTX 5070 for just $2,049 right now — 32GB of fast DDR5 RAM and a 2TB SSD round off powerhouse specs, with an extra $200 off this custom-built AMD rig
content: Save $200 on this awesome AMD build from iBuyPower, featuring an AMD Ryzen 7 7800X3D, RTX 5070, 32GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 2TB SSD, all for just $2,049 right now.

title: Broadcom to supply Anthropic with 3.5 gigawatts of Google TPU capacity from 2027 — Claude pioneer says its annual revenue run rate has passed $30 billion
content: Broadcom disclosed in a securities filing on Monday that it’ll supply Anthropic with roughly 3.5 gigawatts of Google TPU capacity starting in 2027.
